paper_title: Metformin -An agent stimulating motility and acrosome reaction in chicken sperm

entities.description: An agent/chemical (1,1-dimethylbiguanide hydrochloride) used to stimulate sperm motility and acrosome reaction.
The ability of chicken sperm to move spontaneously and actively.
A physiological process in sperm necessary for oocyte fertilization.
Technique for long-term storage of sperm at very low temperatures causing potential damage.
Computer-assisted sperm analysis system used to evaluate sperm motility parameters.
Analytical technique used to detect specific protein phosphorylation (AMPK).
A specific extender or buffer used for diluting semen during cryopreservation.
Percentage increase in fresh sperm motility observed with 1mM Metformin.
Percentage increase in acrosome reaction rate observed with 1mM Metformin.
Increase in motility of cryopreserved sperm treated with Metformin compared to control.
Increase in viability of cryopreserved sperm treated with Metformin.
Measured increase in AMPK activation after Metformin treatment during freeze-thaw.
The primary research area focusing on sperm function and cryopreservation.
Lead author of the study from Quy Nhon University.
Institution located in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.

relations.source: Metformin - An agent stimulating motility and acrosome reaction in chicken sperm
